articles.summary: 发布Seed2.0系列模型，面向真实世界复杂任务。通过识别用户真实需求并构建可靠的前瞻性评估系统，在多项基准上取得进展，旨在推动智能体解决实际复杂问题。

articles.title: Multimodal Continuous Reasoning via Asymmetric Mutual Variational Learning
articles.excerpt: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) are often constrained by a language-space bottleneck, forcing complex visual reasoning into discrete tokens which can lose perceptual nuance. A promising alternative is continuous latent reasoning, where the goal is to discover implicit reasoning pathways tha
articles.summary: 针对多模态大模型语言空间瓶颈（将视觉推理离散化为token损失感知细节），提出非对称互变分学习实现连续潜在推理，在不压缩为离散token的情况下发现隐式推理路径。

articles.title: MemSyco-Bench: Benchmarking Sycophancy in Agent Memory
articles.excerpt: Memory has emerged as a cornerstone of modern LLM-based agents, supporting their evolution from single-turn assistants to long-term collaborators. However, memory is not always beneficial: retrieved memories often induce a critical issue of sycophancy, causing agents to over-align with the user at t
articles.summary: 发现记忆可能导致智能体过度迎合用户（sycophancy）。提出MemSyco-Bench基准，系统评估基于记忆的LLM智能体中的谄媚行为，揭示记忆检索对决策偏差的影响。

articles.title: WorldDirector: Building Controllable World Simulators with Persistent Dynamic Memory
articles.excerpt: We present WorldDirector, a highly controllable video world model framework designed for persistent dynamic object memory and unrestricted viewpoint exploration. Unlike existing world models that entangle physical dynamics with pixel rendering and rely on continuous visual observation to sustain mot
articles.summary: 提出WorldDirector框架，构建可控视频世界模型，支持持久动态物体记忆和自由视角探索。不同于依赖连续视觉观察的现有模型，它将物理动态与像素渲染解耦。

articles.title: ASPIRE: Agentic /Skills Discovery for Robotics
articles.excerpt: Traditional robot programming is challenging: it requires orchestrating multimodal perception, managing physical contact dynamics, and handling diverse configurations and execution failures. We introduce ASPIRE (Agentic Skill Programming through Iterative Robot Exploration), a continual learning sys
articles.summary: 提出ASPIRE（通过迭代机器人探索进行自主技能编程），一个持续学习系统，使机器人自主发现和复用技能。无需手动编程，自动管理多模态感知、接触动力学和故障处理。

articles.title: Breaking Failure Cascades: Step-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Medical Multimodal Reasoning
articles.excerpt: Recent multimodal large language models have shown great promise in clinical image reasoning, but existing post-training pipelines remain predominantly outcome-centric, relying on final answer correctness or sequence-level preferences. This suffers from sparse credit assignment, making it difficult
articles.summary: 针对医学多模态推理中结果中心训练的稀疏奖励问题，提出步骤感知强化学习。通过在每个推理步骤提供细粒度反馈，打破错误级联，提升临床图像推理的准确性。

articles.title: Logit-Contribution Scoring Identifies Non-Literal Retrieval Heads
articles.excerpt: In long-context use, large language models frequently synthesize answers from the meaning of a relevant context span rather than literally copy-pasting them. Identifying which attention heads perform this synthesis matters for interpreting long-context model behavior. Yet existing detectors miss the
articles.summary: 发现LLM在长上下文中常根据含义合成答案而非直接复制。提出Logit贡献评分方法，识别执行非字面检索的注意力头，帮助解释长上下文模型行为。

articles.title: SkillCoach: Self-Evolving Rubrics for Evaluating and Enhancing Agentic Skill-Use
articles.excerpt: Skills are becoming a reusable operational layer for LLM agents, encoding SOPs, domain rules, tool workflows, scripts, and validation routines. In realistic skill repositories, overlapping skills make reliable skill-use difficult. Final verifier success is too coarse for both evaluation and training
articles.summary: 针对技能库中重叠技能导致智能体选择困难的问题，提出SkillCoach框架。自演化的评估准则用于评价和训练智能体的技能使用，优于最终验证器。

articles.title: AutoMem: Automated Learning of Memory as a Cognitive Skill
articles.excerpt: Memory expertise is a learned skill: knowing what to encode, when to retrieve, and how to organize knowledge--a capacity known in cognitive science as metamemory. We bring this perspective to LLMs by treating memory management as a trainable skill. We promote file-system operations to first-class me
articles.summary: 将记忆管理视为可训练技能，受认知科学中元记忆启发。提升文件系统操作为一等公民，使LLM学会何时编码、检索和组织知识，自动化记忆学习。

articles.title: Graph-Native Reinforcement Learning Enables Traceable Scientific Hypothesis Generation through Conceptual Recombination
articles.excerpt: Accelerating materials discovery requires AI systems that can generate scientifically valid hypotheses through multi-step, domain-grounded reasoning. Standard large language models often produce fluent but weakly traceable responses to open-ended materials design problems, making it difficult to det
articles.summary: 针对材料发现中科学假设生成的可追溯性问题，提出图原生强化学习方法。通过概念重组实现多步骤、领域 grounded 推理，生成可追溯的假设，优于标准LLM。

articles.title: InstanceControl: Controllable Complex Image Generation without Instance Labeling
articles.excerpt: Controllable image generation methods, such as ControlNet, have demonstrated a remarkable capacity to introduce visual conditions(e.g., depth maps) to guide image generation. However, these methods often struggle with complex multi-instance scenes, frequently leading to attribute confusion among ins
articles.summary: 针对多实例场景下属性混淆问题，提出InstanceControl方法。无需实例标注，通过条件控制生成复杂图像，解决ControlNet等方法在多实例中的不足。

articles.title: When Search Agents Should Ask: DiscoBench for Clarification-Aware Deep Search
articles.excerpt: Search agents powered by large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to solve complex information-seeking tasks, requiring multi-step retrieval and reasoning to fulfill user goals. However, existing benchmarks often assume that user queries are complete and explicit, overlooking the fact that
articles.summary: 现有搜索基准假设用户查询完整明确，忽略歧义。提出DiscoBench基准，评估搜索智能体何时应该主动提问澄清，从而提升深度信息检索的准确性。

articles.title: The State-Prediction Separation Hypothesis
articles.excerpt: Transformers use the same forward computation stream to both predict the next token and store useful state for future token predictions. We formulate the state-prediction separation hypothesis: disentangling the two roles yields better language modeling performance. We design a Transformer variant t
articles.summary: 提出状态预测分离假说：Transformer同时用于预测下一个token和存储未来状态，分离这两个角色可提升语言建模性能。设计了相应的Transformer变体。

articles.title: AnyGroundBench: A Specialized-Domain Benchmark for Video Grounding in Vision-Language Models
articles.excerpt: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated immense promise in Spatio-Temporal Video Grounding (STVG). However, current evaluation protocols are largely confined to zero-shot assessments on general, daily-life benchmarks. This creates a critical disconnect from real-world applications in special
articles.summary: 现有视频定位评估局限于通用日常场景，缺乏专业领域评估。提出AnyGroundBench基准，覆盖专用领域（如医疗、工业），评估VLM在时空视频定位上的表现。

articles.title: PixelEyes: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Pinpoint Visual Evidence Seeking
articles.excerpt: This paper explores multi-turn visual reasoning and observes that MLLMs repeatedly fail to localize the target, leading to long, redundant trajectories. We attribute this failure to the entanglement of reasoning and perception within a single model, the MLLM reasons and localizes simultaneously, and

articles.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
articles.excerpt: While large language models (LLMs) perform well on table tasks, they still make data referencing errors (DREs), i.e., incorrectly citing or omitting table values, despite understanding the table structure. Beyond final-answer accuracy, DREs directly compromise the correctness and reliability of inte

articles.title: PACE: A Proxy for Agentic Capability Evaluation
articles.excerpt: Evaluating LLM agents on benchmarks like SWE-Bench and GAIA can be expensive, time-consuming, and requires complex infrastructure. A single evaluation can cost thousands of dollars and take days to complete. In contrast, non-agentic LLM benchmarks that test individual capabilities (e.g., reasoning,

articles.title: Are Performance-Optimization Benchmarks Reliably Measuring Coding Agents?
articles.excerpt: Repository-level performance-optimization benchmarks such as GSO, SWE-Perf and SWE-fficiency evaluate coding agents by applying patches to real repositories and comparing runtime against unoptimized baselines and official reference patches. Their leaderboard scores are increasingly used as evidence

articles.title: WARP: Weight-Space Analysis for Recovering Training Data Portfolios
articles.excerpt: Foundation models are routinely released to the public, yet the data recipes used to train them -- such as domain mixture weights that determine how different sources are sampled -- are rarely disclosed. This creates an access asymmetry: researchers study the resulting models but lack visibility int

articles.title: DuoMem: Towards Capable On-Device Memory Agents via Dual-Space Distillation
articles.excerpt: Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents can solve complex procedural tasks by interacting with environments over multiple turns, but this ability typically depends on large models, long contexts, and repeated inference calls. This makes advanced memory-augmented agents difficult to deploy on resourc

articles.title: Building to the Test: Coding Agents Deliver What You Check, Not What You Requested
articles.excerpt: Benchmarks are widely used to evaluate task completion by Large Language Models (LLMs), but this approach has accumulated construction-validity problems, and a passing score may not show whether the requested task was delivered. We study both problems. In a controlled code-as-spec setup, two product

articles.title: AI translation of literary texts is "fine", but readers still prefer human translations
articles.excerpt: AI translation of literary works is increasingly common. articles.excerpt: Diffusion language models, which generate text by denoising a token canvas bidirectionally instead of emitting tokens left to right, have become competitive with autoregressive (AR) generation.
